page: Microsoft Azure L4as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Lasv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. Local storage is bundled with a 960 GB capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core scores, and single-threaded text processing placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance and cryptographic operations score in the average range. However, memory bandwidth for read-only operations is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for both small and large blocks. Large-block write bandwidth and Redis SET operations maintain average performance. Large language model inference speeds are average for small 135M models but drop to weak or poor for medium 7B models.

The L4as_v4 offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing or high-throughput read memory bandwidth. The high resource density of 8 GB RAM per vCPU combined with 960 GB of local storage makes it suitable for memory-heavy applications, caching layers, and lightweight database operations. However, the weak multi-threaded decompression and read-only memory bandwidth present a tradeoff for data-intensive pipelines. Recommended workloads include web serving, text processing, memory-cached applications, and general-purpose tasks requiring moderate compute and high memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure L4as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Lasv4 family, featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of local storage. Benchmark results highlight strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core execution and memory read bandwidth are weaker, falling into the bottom 25%. This hardware configuration offers a qualitative balance of high memory density and local storage capacity without the cost of a larger compute footprint. It is well-suited for memory-cached applications, web serving, text processing, and moderate database workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU resources and high RAM-to-vCPU ratios.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L4as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L4as_v4 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 960 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high memory density, and integrated local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Includes 960 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Suited for memory-cached applications, web serving, and moderate database tasks
